calendar.matters:
Petition (1) To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And Will And To Impose Constructive Trust; (2)
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ross Madden for Petitioner.
Timothy Barteau for Mary Prater (Objector).

The Court stated that it reviewed what has been submitted, that it is not persuaded, that although the Trust was initiated here, the Trustor lived in Modesto for some time prior to dying and that the Successor Trustee lives in Modesto. The Court also stated that no conservator has been appointed and the statute cited does not apply. Mr. Madden argued that there is no current Trustee, that the Trust was always administered in San Francisco and that an elder abuse could be transferred here. The Court stated that the language only applies if a conservator is appointed. The Court dismissed the Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust, etc. without prejudice to it being filed where venue is proper.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition (1) To Determine Validity Of Purported Trust And Will And To Impose Constructive Trust; (2)
Off Calendar May-23-2018 Continued To Jun-20-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Timothy Barteau for Mary Prater (Objector)
Ross Madden for William Day (Petitioner)

Mr. Madden stated the settlors were living in San Francisco when the Trust was executed, the trust property is in San Francisco, and there are no objections to San Francisco venue. Mr. Barteau stated that he does not object to venue, there is one asset in San Francisco to establish San Francisco as proper venue. The Court indicated that the Trust was administered from Modesto, and the property in San Francisco is not a basis for venue. Mr. Madden requested a continuance to allow time to respond re: venue. The Amended Petition to Determine Validity of Purported Trust, etc. is continued to June 20, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for counsel to provide basis that San Francisco is the proper venue or for a petition to transfer to proper location; if neither are filed, the court will dismiss for lack of venue.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
